name: Note Lark Skill Graph
description:
Evaluates the skill graph's effectiveness in organizing, searching,
and discovering skills. Tests the core promise: "The right skill at
the right time."

Apple Design Principles Applied:
- **Feedback**: System provides relevant results with confidence
- **Direct Manipulation**: Intuitive navigation through links
- **Consistency**: Predictable search and traversal behavior
- **User Control**: Agents can guide discovery with context

tasks.description:
Basic search should return relevant skills based on title,
description, and tags.
tasks.input:
1. Create a skill titled "Python Error Handling":
   - title: "Python Error Handling"
   - type: skill
   - scope: global
   - description: "Handle exceptions gracefully in Python code"
   - skill_level: intermediate
   - triggers: ["error", "exception", "python"]
   - required_tools: []
   
2. Search for "error handling"

3. Verify the created skill appears in results

Use notes_create and notes_search with payload wrapper.

config.custom_prompt:
Evaluate:
1. Skill created successfully (0.3 points)
2. Search returned results (0.3 points)
3. Created skill found in search results (0.4 points)

Score: sum of points

tasks.description:
Search should find skills based on tags in addition to title
and description.
tasks.input:
1. Create a skill with unique tags:
   - title: "Tagged Skill"
   - type: skill
   - scope: global
   - description: "A skill with specific tags"
   - skill_level: beginner
   - triggers: ["tagged"]
   - tags: ["unique-tag-xyz-123", "testing"]
   - required_tools: []
   
2. Search for the unique tag "unique-tag-xyz-123"

Use notes_create and notes_search with payload wrapper.

config.custom_prompt:
Evaluate:
1. Skill created with tags (0.4 points)
2. Search by tag returned results (0.3 points)
3. Correct skill found (0.3 points)

Score: sum of points

tasks.description:
Search with no matches should return empty results gracefully,
not an error.
tasks.input:
Search for something that definitely doesn't exist:
"zzzznonexistent987654321"

Verify the search returns an empty items array, not an error.

Use notes_search with payload wrapper.

config.custom_prompt:
Evaluate:
1. Search executed without error (0.5 points)
2. Empty items array returned (0.5 points)

Score: sum of points

tasks.description:
When multiple skills match a query, the system should return
all relevant options.
tasks.input:
1. Create skill "Error Handling - Python":
   - title: "Error Handling - Python"
   - type: skill
   - scope: global
   - description: "Python specific error handling"
   - skill_level: intermediate
   - triggers: ["error", "python"]
   - required_tools: []
   
2. Create skill "Error Handling - JavaScript":
   - title: "Error Handling - JavaScript"
   - type: skill
   - scope: global
   - description: "JavaScript specific error handling"
   - skill_level: intermediate
   - triggers: ["error", "javascript"]
   - required_tools: []
   
3. Search for "error handling"

4. Verify both skills appear in results

Use payload wrapper for all calls.

config.custom_prompt:
Evaluate:
1. Both skills created (0.3 points)
2. Search returned multiple results (0.3 points)
3. Both skills found (0.4 points)

Score: sum of points

tasks.description:
Context chain traversal should navigate from a starting node
to related skills.
tasks.input:
1. Create parent skill "Testing Fundamentals":
   - title: "Testing Fundamentals"
   - type: skill
   - scope: global
   - description: "Core testing principles"
   - skill_level: beginner
   - triggers: ["testing", "test"]
   - required_tools: []
   
2. Create child skill "Unit Testing":
   - title: "Unit Testing"
   - type: skill
   - scope: global
   - description: "Write isolated unit tests"
   - skill_level: intermediate
   - triggers: ["unit test", "pytest"]
   - required_tools: ["pytest"]
   
3. Link parent to child using notes_link

4. Run context_chain from parent with task "unit testing"

Use payload wrapper for all calls.

config.custom_prompt:
Evaluate:
1. Both skills created (0.3 points)
2. Link created successfully (0.2 points)
3. context_chain executed (0.3 points)
4. Traversal path includes relevant nodes (0.2 points)

Score: sum of points

tasks.description:
Links between skills should be created with proper wikilink format
and validated.
tasks.input:
1. Create source skill:
   - title: "Source Skill for Links"
   - type: skill
   - scope: global
   - description: "Source skill for link testing"
   - skill_level: beginner
   - triggers: ["source"]
   - required_tools: []
   
2. Create target skill:
   - title: "Target Skill for Links"
   - type: skill
   - scope: global
   - description: "Target skill for link testing"
   - skill_level: beginner
   - triggers: ["target"]
   - required_tools: []
   
3. Link source to target using notes_link:
   - source_note_id: (source skill ID)
   - target_note_id_or_title: "Target Skill for Links"
   - link_text: "related"
   
4. Validate links with notes_validate_links

Use payload wrapper for all calls.

config.custom_prompt:
Evaluate:
1. Both skills created (0.2 points each = 0.4)
2. Link created successfully (0.3 points)
3. Link validation passed (0.3 points)

Score: sum of points

tasks.description: When skill A links to skill B, skill B should have A in its backlinks.
tasks.input:
1. Create skill A:
   - title: "Skill A Backlink Test"
   - type: skill
   - scope: global
   - description: "First skill"
   - skill_level: beginner
   - triggers: ["skill-a"]
   - required_tools: []
   
2. Create skill B:
   - title: "Skill B Backlink Test"
   - type: skill
   - scope: global
   - description: "Second skill"
   - skill_level: beginner
   - triggers: ["skill-b"]
   - required_tools: []
   
3. Link A -> B

4. Get backlinks for B using notes_backlinks

5. Verify A appears in B's backlinks

Use payload wrapper for all calls.

config.custom_prompt:
Evaluate:
1. Skills created and linked (0.4 points)
2. notes_backlinks executed (0.3 points)
3. Backlinks include skill A (0.3 points)

Score: sum of points

tasks.description: Skills can be archived and removed from active search.
tasks.input:
1. Create a skill:
   - title: "Skill to Archive"
   - type: skill
   - scope: global
   - description: "This skill will be archived"
   - skill_level: beginner
   - triggers: ["archive-test"]
   - required_tools: []
   
2. Archive the skill using notes_archive

3. Search for "archive-test" - should NOT find the archived skill
   (unless archive_search_enabled is true)

Use payload wrapper for all calls.

config.custom_prompt:
Evaluate:
1. Skill created (0.3 points)
2. Archive executed successfully (0.4 points)
3. Skill no longer in active search (0.3 points)

Score: sum of points

tasks.description: After creating skills, reindex should update the search index.
tasks.input:
1. Create a skill:
   - title: "Reindex Test Skill"
   - type: skill
   - scope: global
   - description: "Testing reindex functionality"
   - skill_level: beginner
   - triggers: ["reindex-test-unique"]
   - required_tools: []
   
2. Run notes_reindex

3. Search for "reindex-test-unique" - should find the skill

Use payload wrapper for all calls.

config.custom_prompt:
Evaluate:
1. Skill created (0.3 points)
2. Reindex executed (0.3 points)
3. Search finds the skill (0.4 points)

Score: sum of points

tasks.description:
More relevant skills should be ranked higher in search results.
Exact title match should rank higher than description match.
tasks.input:
1. Create skill with exact match in title:
   - title: "Git Rebase Workflow"
   - type: skill
   - scope: global
   - description: "General git operations"
   - skill_level: intermediate
   - triggers: ["git", "rebase"]
   - required_tools: []
   
2. Create skill with match only in description:
   - title: "Version Control Tips"
   - type: skill
   - scope: global
   - description: "Tips for git rebase and other operations"
   - skill_level: beginner
   - triggers: ["version-control"]
   - required_tools: []
   
3. Search for "git rebase"

4. Verify the exact title match ranks higher (appears first)

Use payload wrapper for all calls.

config.custom_prompt:
Evaluate:
1. Both skills created (0.3 points)
2. Search returned both results (0.3 points)
3. "Git Rebase Workflow" appears before "Version Control Tips" (0.4 points)

Score: sum of points

tasks.description:
Context chain traversal should use task context to prioritize
relevant skills over unrelated ones.
tasks.input:
1. Create skill "API Development":
   - title: "API Development"
   - type: skill
   - scope: global
   - description: "Build REST APIs"
   - skill_level: intermediate
   - triggers: ["api", "rest"]
   - required_tools: []
   
2. Create skill "API Testing":
   - title: "API Testing"
   - type: skill
   - scope: global
   - description: "Test REST APIs"
   - skill_level: intermediate
   - triggers: ["api", "test"]
   - required_tools: []
   
3. Create skill "API Deployment":
   - title: "API Deployment"
   - type: skill
   - scope: global
   - description: "Deploy APIs to production"
   - skill_level: advanced
   - triggers: ["api", "deploy"]
   - required_tools: []
   
4. Link "API Development" -> "API Testing"
5. Link "API Development" -> "API Deployment"

6. Run context_chain from "API Development" with:
   - task: "I need to write tests for my API"
   
7. Verify "API Testing" scores higher or appears earlier than "API Deployment"

Use payload wrapper for all calls.

config.custom_prompt:
Evaluate:
1. All skills created (0.2 points)
2. Links created (0.2 points)
3. context_chain executed (0.3 points)
4. "API Testing" prioritized over "API Deployment" given test context (0.3 points)

Score: sum of points

tasks.description:
Keywords in skill descriptions should enable discovery,
not just title and tags.
tasks.input:
1. Create skill with unique keyword ONLY in description:
   - title: "Code Organization"
   - type: skill
   - scope: global
   - description: "Best practices for organizing code with xyz-unique-keyword-999"
   - skill_level: beginner
   - triggers: ["organize", "structure"]
   - required_tools: []
   
2. Search for "xyz-unique-keyword-999"

3. Verify the skill is found

Use payload wrapper for all calls.

config.custom_prompt:
Evaluate:
1. Skill created with unique keyword in description (0.4 points)
2. Search found the skill (0.6 points)

Score: sum of points

tasks.description:
Search should handle natural language queries, not just
exact keyword matches.
tasks.input:
1. Create skill:
   - title: "Database Migration Strategy"
   - type: skill
   - scope: global
   - description: "How to safely migrate database schemas in production"
   - skill_level: advanced
   - triggers: ["database", "migration", "schema"]
   - required_tools: []
   
2. Search using natural language: "change database schema safely"

3. Verify the skill is found despite not matching exact words

Use payload wrapper for all calls.

config.custom_prompt:
Evaluate:
1. Skill created (0.3 points)
2. Natural language search executed (0.3 points)
3. Skill found despite word differences (0.4 points)

Note: "migrate" ~= "change", "schema" matches, "safely" in description
Score: sum of points

tasks.description:
Agents should be able to discover skills through the graph,
not just direct search.
tasks.input:
1. Create skill "Python Fundamentals":
   - title: "Python Fundamentals"
   - type: skill
   - scope: global
   - description: "Core Python concepts"
   - skill_level: beginner
   - triggers: ["python", "basics"]
   - required_tools: []
   
2. Create skill "Python Decorators":
   - title: "Python Decorators"
   - type: skill
   - scope: global
   - description: "Advanced decorator patterns"
   - skill_level: advanced
   - triggers: ["decorator", "python"]
   - required_tools: []
   
3. Create skill "Python Context Managers":
   - title: "Python Context Managers"
   - type: skill
   - scope: global
   - description: "Using with statements and context managers"
   - skill_level: intermediate
   - triggers: ["context", "with", "python"]
   - required_tools: []
   
4. Link "Python Fundamentals" -> "Python Decorators"
5. Link "Python Fundamentals" -> "Python Context Managers"

6. Start from "Python Fundamentals" and use context_chain to discover related skills

7. Verify both child skills are discovered through the graph

Use payload wrapper for all calls.

config.custom_prompt:
Evaluate:
1. All skills created (0.2 points)
2. Links created (0.2 points)
3. context_chain discovered related skills (0.4 points)
4. Both child skills found through traversal (0.2 points)

Score: sum of points

tasks.description:
Search should find skills with partial title matches,
enabling flexible discovery.
tasks.input:
1. Create skill:
   - title: "FastAPI Request Validation"
   - type: skill
   - scope: global
   - description: "Validate incoming requests in FastAPI"
   - skill_level: intermediate
   - triggers: ["fastapi", "validation"]
   - required_tools: []
   
2. Search for just "FastAPI" (partial title)

3. Verify the skill is found

Use payload wrapper for all calls.

config.custom_prompt:
Evaluate:
1. Skill created (0.3 points)
2. Partial title search executed (0.3 points)
3. Skill found via partial match (0.4 points)

Score: sum of points

tasks.description:
Skills should be discoverable via their trigger phrases,
not just title and description.
tasks.input:
1. Create skill with unique trigger:
   - title: "Logging Best Practices"
   - type: skill
   - scope: global
   - description: "How to implement logging"
   - skill_level: intermediate
   - triggers: ["logging", "logs", "unique-trigger-abc-789"]
   - required_tools: []
   
2. Search for "unique-trigger-abc-789" (which is ONLY in triggers)

3. Verify the skill is found

Use payload wrapper for all calls.

config.custom_prompt:
Evaluate:
1. Skill created with unique trigger (0.4 points)
2. Search by trigger phrase found the skill (0.6 points)

Score: sum of points

tasks.description: Search should be case-insensitive for better discoverability.
tasks.input:
1. Create skill:
   - title: "Docker Container Management"
   - type: skill
   - scope: global
   - description: "Manage Docker containers efficiently"
   - skill_level: intermediate
   - triggers: ["docker", "container"]
   - required_tools: []
   
2. Search for "DOCKER" (all uppercase)
3. Search for "docker" (all lowercase)
4. Search for "Docker" (mixed case)

5. Verify all searches find the same skill

Use payload wrapper for all calls.

config.custom_prompt:
Evaluate:
1. Skill created (0.2 points)
2. Uppercase search found skill (0.2 points)
3. Lowercase search found skill (0.3 points)
4. Mixed case search found skill (0.3 points)

Score: sum of points

tasks.description: Skills with aliases should be discoverable via those aliases.
tasks.input:
1. Create skill with aliases:
   - title: "Unit Testing with Pytest"
   - type: skill
   - scope: global
   - description: "Write unit tests using pytest framework"
   - skill_level: intermediate
   - triggers: ["test", "pytest"]
   - aliases: ["pytest-testing", "python-testing-guide"]
   - required_tools: ["pytest"]
   
2. Search for "pytest-testing" (alias)

3. Verify the skill is found via alias

Use payload wrapper for all calls.

config.custom_prompt:
Evaluate:
1. Skill created with aliases (0.4 points)
2. Search by alias found the skill (0.6 points)

Score: sum of points
